title: Anthropic Calls for "Global AI Pause"
summary: Wes Roth covers Anthropic's call for a global AI pause as part of broader AI industry news coverage. The segment discusses recent developments from major AI organizations including OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, NVIDIA, and open source initiatives.
writeup: A rapid-fire AI news digest covering three converging signals: the biosecurity letter signed by Sam Altman, Dario Amodei, Demis Hassabis, and dozens of others calling on Congress to mandate screening of synthetic nucleic acid orders; Anthropic's collaboration with the NSA on Claude for cybersecurity (offensive use unconfirmed but not ruled out); and Anthropic's recursive self-improvement (RSI) blog post documenting that Claude Opus 4.6 can now autonomously complete tasks equivalent to 12 hours of human engineering work, with code output per contributor at Anthropic roughly 4x after Mythos preview. The through-line is that capability acceleration is no longer hypothetical: the METR benchmark curve has gone vertical on a log scale, an anonymous Anthropic researcher describes feeling obsolete, and the lab CEOs are treating biosecurity as a present-tense emergency rather than a future one.
key_points: Altman, Amodei, Hassabis, Mustafa Suleyman, Paul Graham, and biotech founders including Twist Biosciences and Ansa Biotechnologies co-signed a letter to Congress calling for mandatory screening and recordkeeping of synthetic nucleic acid orders, citing that current AI systems outperform PhD-level virologists on highly technical laboratory procedures.

Anthropic is working with the NSA under a defensive-use framing, but the published reporting leaves it unclear whether offensive cyber operations are also involved, a distinction the video flags but cannot resolve from available sources.

The METR benchmark of AI agent task automation, measured in equivalent human-hours, shows a clear steepening of the curve starting with 2024 reasoning models: Claude Opus 4.6 handles 12-hour tasks, and a Claude Mythos preview is reported to handle at least 16-hour tasks, doubling the horizon in a matter of months.

Anthropic's RSI blog post documents that Mythos preview raised code merged per active contributor roughly 4x across 130 researchers, and frames the next milestone as agents closing the loop by building and training future model versions autonomously.

An anonymous Anthropic researcher quoted in the RSI blog post describes days when everything works as feeling like personal irrelevance, and days when things break as being incomprehensible, a first-person account of working inside a superhuman AI-driven process.

Adam Bmith of AI Digest is credited as the first commentator to identify the visible steepening of the METR curve when switching from a log to a linear scale, where the exponential acceleration becomes unambiguous.
